summary: Iteration 6 retest of the portal-fix for the mobile drawer backdrop + the asyncio.to_thread wrap of the synchronous SMTP send. ALL acceptance criteria PASS. Backend: 7/7 pytest tests pass including test_contact_smtp_verify_live_delivery (the iteration-6 verify body name='portal fix test', email='hello@trackthebreach.com', reason='Sales', message='verifying portal fix did not break SMTP' was POSTed to /api/contact and returned 200 with email_sent:true — real email delivered via Gmail SMTP through asyncio.to_thread; persisted to MongoDB contact_submissions). Invalid email still returns 422. Frontend mobile (390x844): mobile-menu and mobile-menu-backdrop are now children of document.body (closest('header') === null, parent.tagName === 'BODY' for both). Backdrop renders persistently in DOM, with opacity transition (opacity-100 + pointer-events-auto when open, opacity-0 + pointer-events-none when closed) — no more mount/unmount. When drawer is open, backdrop getBoundingClientRect() = {x:0, y:64, width:390, height:780} (covers full viewport below the 64px header), opacity=1, position=fixed, z-index=40. Real mouse click via page.mouse.click(195, 502) on the backdrop area below the drawer card closed the drawer: backdrop_opacity 1→0, drawer_opacity 1→0, body_overflow 'hidden'→'' confirmed. Accordion: all 4 sections collapsed on open (aria-expanded=false), single-open behaviour preserved (opening solutions auto-collapses product). Bottom CTAs present: mobile-pricing, mobile-contact, mobile-login, mobile-signup. Mobile header layout: hamburger at x<30 (left), brand centerX≈195 (viewport center). Body scroll lock works (document.body.style.overflow='hidden' on open, restored to '' on close). Desktop regression on /pricing: 'Pricing built to defend you' headline present; all 5 pricing-plan-0..4 cards show 'one-time' + 'Credits never expire' badges; footer-pct-link text='People's Choice Tech' href='https://peopleschoice.tech/' target='_blank'; desktop nav-pricing className contains 'text-[15px]' + 'font-semibold'. No new bugs introduced by the portal change or the asyncio.to_thread wrap.
action_items: Optional (carried from iter-5, not blocking): add rate limiting to /api/contact (e.g., slowapi @limiter.limit('3/minute')) before going public — live SMTP send on every POST is currently abusable as outbound spam through your Gmail account.

Optional (carried from iter-5): expose email_error in the 200 response body when SMTP fails so the UI can show a fallback CTA; currently the FE has no signal that the email leg failed (it would just see email_sent:false).
critical_code_review_comments: Navbar.jsx: portal implementation is correct and minimal — drawer + backdrop wrapped in createPortal(<>...</>, document.body), and typeof document !== 'undefined' guards SSR. Good. The backdrop uses opacity transition + pointer-events toggle (instead of conditional rendering), so the DOM node is stable across open/close — eliminates the mount race that hurt iter-5. No regressions detected.

server.py: _send_email() correctly wrapped in `await asyncio.to_thread(_send_email, submission)` — the FastAPI event loop is no longer blocked by the 200-2000ms SMTP handshake. Backend keeps serving concurrent requests during email delivery. Good.
server.py: contact_submissions docs now also include 'email_error' field when SMTP raises — but no API surface exposes it. Consider returning a sanitized hint to the FE for better UX.
Still no per-IP throttling on /api/contact — flagged in iter-5, still applies. Treat as REQUIRED before public launch.

success_rate.backend: 100% (7/7 pytest, incl. live SMTP delivery via asyncio.to_thread)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (portal fix + accordion + CTAs + desktop regressions all pass)

test_credentials: N/A — no auth on this site. SMTP creds (hello@trackthebreach.com + App Password) live in /app/backend/.env.
seed_data_creation: Test docs inserted into contact_submissions with name prefix 'TEST_' + one 'portal fix test' doc — all auto-cleaned by module-scoped fixture teardown (delete_many on TEST_ regex, 'SMTP Verify Test', and 'portal fix test').
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 6 closes the iter-5 HIGH bug: mobile drawer backdrop is now visible (height=780, opacity=1) and clickable (real pointer events close drawer). Portal mount confirmed (parent=document.body, closest('header')=null). SMTP send is now non-blocking (asyncio.to_thread). All iteration-3/4/5 regressions still pass (pricing layout, headline copy, one-time + Credits never expire on all 5 plans, PCT footer link, desktop navbar font, hamburger-left/brand-center on mobile, body scroll lock, single-open accordion). No new bugs introduced. Outstanding non-blocking items: rate-limit /api/contact and surface email_error to FE for better UX.
rca_of_the_issue: Iter-5 RCA was: backdrop inside <header> with backdrop-filter became a containing block for its fixed descendant, collapsing the backdrop to height:0. Fix verified: by portaling the backdrop+drawer into document.body via React.createPortal, the fixed positioning now resolves against the viewport (not the header), producing a 390x780 backdrop that receives real pointer events. SMTP RCA addendum: smtplib.SMTP is synchronous and the original handler `await`-ed nothing, so a single slow handshake blocked the FastAPI worker. Fix verified: `await asyncio.to_thread(_send_email, submission)` offloads the blocking call to the default threadpool while leaving the route async — pytest confirms still 200 + email_sent:true.
